title: age-group — model relationships
description: AgeGroup is a leaf reference-data aggregate below Tenant with no children of its own inside this module. Optional FK to Organization (nullable — when null the row is tenant-wide; when set the row is scoped to one Organization's brand). Inbound relations arrive from Team (Wave 2b, nullable FK) and AthleteEnrollment (Wave 3+, snapshotted at enrollment time so the enrollment survives athlete birthday drift). Every FK from an AgeGroup to another aggregate is same-tenant — cross-tenant FKs are forbidden per `.kiro/steering/tenancy-columns.md` §5. `age_groups` NEVER carries `application_id` (hierarchy.md §2 forbids it — Application cascades through `tenants.application_id`), NEVER carries `region_id` (age groups don't scope to regions; the same U12 group applies across every region of a tenant), NEVER carries `branch_id` (same — U12 applies across every branch), NEVER carries `scope_node_id` (age groups are not a scope consumer).

outbound.description: The tenant that owns this age group. CASCADE aligns with tenancy's TenantErased contract — every age group is same-tenant scoped. The tenant-erasure hook (`PreventAgeGroupOrphansOnTenantErased`) does NOT rehome age groups to another tenant (cross-tenant age groups are forbidden); it just lets the cascade fire after every dependent row (Team, AthleteEnrollment) has already been erased.

OPTIONAL — nullable = tenant-wide age group (applies to every Organization). Non-null = scoped to one Organization (a tenant running multiple brands may define different U12 rules per brand — e.g. 'Elite U12' vs 'Recreational U12'). RESTRICT because deleting an Organization while it owns non-tenant-wide age groups is a data-quality error; the Organization module's own erasure flow rehomes or archives them first.

inbound.description: Domain models that reference an age group compose the `BelongsToAgeGroup` trait shipped here. They store the linkage as an `age_group_id` FK. FKs are nullable at Wave 2a (before Team + AthleteEnrollment land); Wave 2b will start populating Team.age_group_id, Wave 3+ will start snapshotting AthleteEnrollment.age_group_snapshot_id.
examples: teams::Team.age_group_id — nullable (Wave 2b). A Team may or may not carry an age-group classifier; teams for open-age competitions may leave it null.

athlete-enrollment::AthleteEnrollment.age_group_snapshot_id — nullable (Wave 3+). SNAPSHOTTED at enrollment time so the enrollment survives athlete birthday drift + age-group bounds changes. The snapshot column stores the age_group_id + a JSONB copy of the bounds so if the underlying AgeGroup is edited or deleted, the enrollment's original eligibility posture stays intact.
event::EventDivision.age_group_id — nullable (Wave 3+). Competition divisions filter Team registration by age group.
team-registration::TeamRegistration.eligibility_age_group_id — nullable (Wave 3+). Enforces registrant eligibility at signup time.
inbound.onDelete: RESTRICT by convention on domain FKs referencing age_groups.id — the AgeGroupObserver refuses to delete an AgeGroup while ANY inbound row (Team, AthleteEnrollment, EventDivision) still references it. The user-facing error is `AGE_GROUP_IN_USE` (409). Snapshotted references (AthleteEnrollment.age_group_snapshot_id) do NOT block delete — snapshots are self-contained copies + do not RESTRICT the source row.

morphs.name: scopeNode
morphs.description: Every AgeGroup projects into the scope substrate as a node under the tenant root — enabling per-AgeGroup configuration overrides on the config-consumer modules (settings, notifications, entitlements). NOT a scope consumer itself: AgeGroup rows never carry `scope_node_id` (per `.kiro/steering/tenancy-columns.md` §4). The scope node references the AgeGroup via `entity_id`; the reverse lookup happens through this morph.

notes: AgeGroup is orthogonal to Region + Branch. It carries neither FK. An age group like 'U12' applies uniformly across every region + every branch of a tenant — the eligibility rule is the same whether the athlete plays in Dubai or Riyadh, and whether they train at the Downtown branch or the Marina branch.

Age groups may be scoped by Organization (nullable FK) because tenants running multiple sub-brands sometimes need different eligibility rules per brand — e.g. 'Elite Academy U12' with a tighter age cutoff for high-performance streams vs 'Community Program U12' with a broader window. Region + branch scoping was considered but rejected — those axes carry commercial/venue concerns, not sport-eligibility concerns.

The forbidden columns per `tenancy-columns.md` §5 are ENFORCED at review + by the tenancy-compliance-auditor: `application_id` on age_groups is a schema violation; `region_id` on age_groups is a schema violation; `branch_id` on age_groups is a schema violation; `scope_node_id` on age_groups is a schema violation.

AgeGroup does not carry `parent_id` — age groups are flat per (tenant, organization, sport_key) tuple. There's no 'U12 is a child of Junior' hierarchy; the numeric bounds already establish the ordering. Age-group programmes that legitimately nest (e.g. 'Junior Program' → 'U12', 'U14') live in a future `programme` aggregate that owns its own hierarchy + references age groups by FK.
